architecture.summary: LFEAR combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with an auto-regressive fine-tuned language model in a conversation format. The system retrieves industry-specific contextual information and feeds it alongside the input text into a fine-tuned auto-regressive model to perform sentiment analysis across multiple domains (reviews of restaurants, movies, politics, financial products). The model is fine-tuned on Hellopeter reviews to capture domain-specific language patterns and overcome limitations of lexicon-based sentiment tools.
trained_components: RAG retrieval component for domain-specific context

biblio.title: Fine-Tuning Retrieval-Augmented Generation with an Auto-Regressive Language Model for Sentiment Analysis in Financial Reviews

claims.statement: The LFEAR model achieved an average precision score of 98.45%
answer correctness of 93.85% based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation Assessment (RAGAS) metrics
context precision of 97.69% based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation Assessment (RAGAS) metrics
LFEAR is effective in conducting sentiment analysis across various domains (restaurants, movies, politics, financial products) due to its adaptability and scalable inference mechanism

baseline.name: lexicon-based tools and pre-defined sentiment lexicons

claims.statement: LFEAR overcomes limitations of lexicon-based tools and the reliance on pre-defined sentiment lexicons, which may not fully capture the range of sentiments in natural language

Pre-trained larger language models (PTLMs) struggle to capture bidirectional contextual knowledge learned through word dependency because sentence-level representation fails to take broad features into account
